sources.translation: ‘To Ision and the other nine superintendents of the lease of the dyeing monopoly, from Heron son of Herodes son of Theogiton, of the quarter of the temple of Sekneptunis. I wish to be granted by you the control of the weaving in the village of Archelais for the present 13th year only of Aurelius Antoninus Caesar the lord at a total yearly rent of 300 silver drachmae which I will pay in equal monthly instalments, the fiscal charges payable for other accounts falling upon me the lessee, if you consent to the concession. (Signed) I, Isidorus, have received a copy of this application. I, Ision, have received a copy of this application. The 13th year of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar the lord, Thoth 2[.].’

sources.translation:
49. To Harpokration also called Hierax, basilikos grammateus of the Arsinoite nome, Herakleides division, from Herodes son of Heron son of Herakleides, mother Eirene, from the metropolis, registered in the quarter of Tameia.

I own in the quarter of Bithynoi Allon Topon a tenth share of a house, in which I dwell, and I register myself and my household for the house-by-house census of the past 28th year of Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Caesar the lord [---]. I registered [previously].

I am Herodes, the aforementioned, subject to the poll-tax, weaver, aged 50; and my wife who is also my sister, Eirene, aged 54; and our children: Heron [---], aged 29; and Neilos, another son, goldsmith, aged 25; and Sarapion, registered among those born since the last census, aged [---]; and Herakleides, aged 9; and Euporas, aged 7, both not yet registered among those born since the last census; and [---], aged 23; and Neilliaina, wife of Heron, aged [---]; and Thaisarion, aged 17; and the children of Heron and Neilliaina: Herodes and Tryphon, both twins, not yet registered among those born since the last census, aged 1; and the wife of Neilos: Thermoutharion daughter of Kastor son of Heron, mother Isidora, from the metropolis, aged 29; and their children: [---], aged 13; and Heron, aged [---], both not yet registered among those born since the last census.

And the children of my deceased brother Herakleides: Heron, mother Eirene, rod-maker, aged 34; and Apion, mother the same, laborer, aged 24; and Herakleides, goldsmith, aged 19; and Thaisarion, wife of Heron, aged 17; and their daughter Syra, aged 1.

And tenants: Neilos son of Demetrios son of [---], mother Thaisarion, subject to the poll-tax, donkey-driver, aged 44; and his wife who is also his sister, Eirene, aged 52; and their son Kastor, not yet registered among those born since the last census, aged 8; and the brothers of the aforementioned Thermoutharion, of the same father and mother: Heron, rod-maker, subject to the poll-tax, aged 34; and Melas, gardener, aged 32; and Heron son of Herakleides son of Heron, mother Didyme, subject to the poll-tax, laborer, aged 26; and his sister of the same father and mother, [---], aged 23.

All the foregoing were previously registered with me in the census of the 14th year of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus in the aforementioned quarter of Tameia. Therefore I submit this declaration.

I, Herodes, have submitted it. Harpokrat[ion ---]. Year 29 of Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Caesar the lord [---].

[Second declaration:] To Harpokration also called Hierax, basilikos grammateus of the Arsinoite nome, Herakleides division, from Sarapammon son of Apollonios son of [---], mother Didymarion, katoikos of the 6475(?), from the metropolis, registered in the quarter of Tameia.

I own in the quarter of Moiris near the gate [---] a new house and an open court and a courtyard, in which I dwell, and I register myself and my household for the house-by-house census of the past 28th year of Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Caesar the lord, in the same quarter of Tameia, in which also in the census of the 14th year I was registered.

I am Sarapammon, the aforementioned, katoikos who has passed the epikrisis, aged 70; and my slave Aunes, purchased by me in year [---] in the same quarter of Tameia [---] registered in the census of the 14th year by his former masters, who sold him to me: as to one-third, Kronion son of Kronion son of Herakleides, mother [---], and his brothers, in the same quarter of Tameia; and as to the remaining two-thirds, Hero[---] also called Tourbon son of Heron, mother [---], and his brothers, in the quarter of Gymnasion.

And my daughter Didymarion was registered by [---] Achilleus son of Apollonios in the quarter of Gymnasion, together with the sons born to them: Valerios and [---].

Therefore I submit this declaration. I, Sarapammon son of Apollonios, have submitted it. I, Tyrannos son of Heron, wrote on his behalf as he is illiterate.

Year 29 of Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Caesar the lord, Pauni. Tameia.

sources.translation:
Translation: from P.Oxy. 7
              To Claudius Menandrus, basilicogrammateus, from Teos, younger son of Onnophris son of Teos, his mother being Taseus, and Asklas son of Onnophris son of Osmolchis, his mother being Tesauris, both of the city of Oxyrhynchus, hieroglyphic carvers, who have been delegated by their fellow-carvers: the list of ourselves and the said fellow-carvers of hieroglyphics for the present 11th year of Trajanus Caesar the lord, as follows :—
              In the quarter of the Tenth, Teos son of Onnophris, the aforesaid, Onnophris his brother, Asklas son of Onnophris, the aforesaid, Osmolchis his brother, who is also a hieroglyphic carver of Osiris the most great god.
              In the quarter of the square of Thoeris, Ptolemaeus son of Petosorapis son of Petosorapis.
              Total 5 men. And we swear by the Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus that we have honestly and truthfully presented the foregoing list, and that there are no more than these, and that we have no apprentices or strangers carrying on the art down to the present day, otherwise may we be liable to the consequences of the oath. The 11th year of the Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, Phaophi 29.

Translation: from P.Oxy. 3
              Hatres to Heras his brother, greeting. In accordance with your instructions concerning Serenus the fuller who is working with Phileas, if you have need of him send a servant for him to-day, the 19th. Do not neglect this, as I am keeping him. I pray for your health and prosperity. (Addressed) Deliver to Heras.

sources.translation: [This is a land register from Theadelphia, late 2nd/early 3rd century CE. It lists landholders and their holdings in arouras: Serenos, former anti-exegetes, 3 1/2 arouras; Theoninos, linen-weaver, 1 1/2 arouras; Sarapodoros, copper-vessel dealer and scholarch, 3 arouras; Aphrodisios son of Orsenouphis, 3 arouras; Sabeinos, former gymnasiarch, 2 arouras; Seloura, wife of Petermouthes, 2 arouras; Heron son of Hermeinos, 4 arouras; Tyrrannis also called Kopreia and Sarapias [...], 1 3/4 arouras; wife of G[...] from Philagris, 11 1/4 arouras; Alexandros and Sarapias, 13 arouras; Hortesios [...], 20 arouras; [land of] Isis the greatest goddess, Ponti[kos...]]

sources.translation: Dionysios and Demaros, catoecic land: arourai 11. K[...]allanos: arourai 2 1/2. Gaius Valerius [...] and Philoxenos: arourai 2. Melas, son of Ptollas: arourai 3. Mysthes son of Palaios, council[...]: arourai 4. Dionysios, son of Ptol[...]: arourai 3. Perseus, weaver: arourai 3 1/2. Heirs of Apollonios son of Phaseis: arourai 6. sources.translation:
The fourth year, the 8th Mesore. Hierax, son of Psonsneou, weaver.
(Demotic) Hierax son of Psonsneus, weaver. The fourth yew, the fourth month of imw (i.e. Mesore), the eighth day.
